name: Los Angeles Unified School District
about: Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) is the second-largest school district in the United States, serving approximately 420,000 students across more than 1,000 schools in the Los Angeles area.
calendarNotes: LAUSD starts the school year in mid-August and operates a traditional semester calendar district-wide, but it also has a significant number of year-round schools — if your child attends a year-round campus, their calendar will differ substantially from this page. Spring Break in LAUSD typically falls in early-to-mid April, later than Texas and most Southeastern states, making it a less congested travel period for popular destinations. The district observes Cesar Chavez Day on March 31 as a school holiday, which is not commonly observed outside California. Winter Break runs about two weeks. With over 1,000 schools spread across a vast metro area, double-check your child's specific school calendar, as site-specific schedules can occasionally vary from the district standard.
